economic_notes: Gas Bar and Convenience Store Expansion underway with $5,561,175 invested as at March 31, 2023. New Health Facility construction managed by Oodanookatoh Inc. with $6,759,751 invested. Section 95 CMHC Housing project (6 units) under construction with $528,812 invested. First Peoples Economic Growth Fund loans suggest community economic development activity.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Five consolidated entities including VLT Program, Health Services, Convenience Store and Gas Bar, and Housing Authority. Capital Investment restricted cash dropped significantly from $2,286,558 to $181,032 indicating draw-down for new health centre construction. Construction in progress nearly doubled to $12,849,739 reflecting Gas Bar expansion and New Health Facility. ISC reconciliation note references 2022/2021 years rather than 2023/2022 — likely a labelling error in the document. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Community and Economic Development segment had very large revenue increase to $6,017,171 from $3,351,146 prior year. Long-term debt includes new First Peoples Economic Growth Fund loans totalling $1,300,000.
